_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Actively looking for ways to help people.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of self-service data platform design principles, including data marketplace concepts, data catalog usability, access request workflows, and user experience considerations that drive adoption.
comments: Weak functional overlap. Service orientation as a disposition is not directly defined in WIDAI. WIDAI covers service delivery (self-service platforms, data product design, service catalogs) but not the underlying service mindset. GAP SIGNAL: WIDAI lacks a service orientation or customer-centricity ability.
_comment_atlas_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: Weak similarity (nist band: much more dissimilar than similar). The moderate STS score reflects a genuine but bounded intersection. The overlapping concepts are detectable but represent a subset of what each description covers independently.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: F1 of 0.8553 indicates lower token-level semantic alignment. Precision exceeds Recall, indicating that the FDE's semantic content is more tightly aligned with the KSA than the reverse — the KSA contains concepts or specificity beyond what the FDE covers. For an Intersects relationship, the P/R balance indicates which direction the overlap tilts — whether the FDE captures more of the KSA's content or vice versa.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: Neutral-dominant (99.68%) — the model finds the descriptions related but neither implying nor contradicting each other. Neutral is the expected NLI outcome for Intersects relationships — the descriptions share conceptual territory without one implying the other.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: The bi-encoder score (0.1447) is moderately lower than the STS cross-encoder (0.2869). The gap (0.14) indicates the relationship has a cross-abstraction component — some of the semantic alignment requires joint processing to detect, suggesting vocabulary or scope divergence between the two descriptions.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Minimal lexical overlap (0.00% of tokens shared). Low lexical overlap combined with low STS indicates both surface vocabulary and underlying semantics diverge between these descriptions.
